subject: ap-precalculus
title: Logarithmic Modeling: Video Game Sales & Average Rate of Change
stimulus: On the initial day of sales ($t = 0$) for a new video game, there were 40 thousand units of the game sold that day. Ninety-one days later ($t = 91$), there were 76 thousand units sold that day. The number of units sold on a given day can be modeled by the function $G$ given by $G(t) = a + b\ln(t + 1)$, where $G(t)$ is the number of units sold, in thousands, on day $t$ since the initial day of sales.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the given data to write two equations that can be used to find the values for constants $a$ and $b$ in the expression for $G(t)$.

(ii) Find the values for $a$ and $b$ as decimal approximations.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Two equations involving $a$ and $b$ using the given input-output pairs, e.g., $a + b\ln(0 + 1) = 40$ and $a + b\ln(91 + 1) = 76$
1 pt: Correct values $a = 40$ and $b = 7.961$ (or $b = 7.961451\ldots$); may be presented as standalone values or in an expression for $G(t)$
parts.scoring_notes: Decimal approximations must be correct to three decimal places. Second point earned with or without supporting work if correct values are identified. A response using 40,000 and 76,000 (correct "thousands" translation) is eligible: $a = 40{,}000$, $b = 7961$. Partial credit (scored 1–0) available if one correct equation is present with one correct value.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the given data to find the average rate of change of the number of units of the video game sold, in thousands per day, from $t = 0$ to $t = 91$ days. Express your answer as a decimal approximation. Show the computations that lead to your answer.

(ii) Use the average rate of change found in (i) to estimate the number of units of the video game sold, in thousands, on day $t = 50$. Show the work that leads to your answer.

(iii) Let $A_t$ represent the estimate of the number of units sold, in thousands, using the average rate of change found in (i). For $A_{50}$ found in (ii), it can be shown that $A_{50} < G(50)$. Explain why, in general, $A_t < G(t)$ for all $t$ where $0 < t < 91$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct average rate of change $\frac{G(91) - G(0)}{91 - 0} = \frac{76 - 40}{91} = 0.396$ (or $0.395$) thousand units per day, in presence of a quotient using the given data values
1 pt: Correct estimate for $t = 50$: $y = 40 + 0.395604 \cdot (50 - 0) = 59.780$ thousand, in presence of supporting work (accept range $[59.750, 59.805]$)
1 pt: Explanation that (a) $G$ is concave down on $(0, 91)$ OR the rate of change of $G$ is decreasing, AND (b) reference to the secant line on $0 < t < 91$ being below the graph of $G$
parts.scoring_notes: Supporting work is required for (i) and (ii). Units are not needed in (i). The final answer in (ii) may be reported as 59 thousand or 60 thousand if supporting work shows a correct decimal approximation. For (iii), both the concavity/rate-of-change statement AND the secant line reference are required to earn the point.

parts.prompt: The makers of the video game reported that daily sales of the video game decreased each day after $t = 91$. Explain why the error in the model $G$ increases after $t = 91$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Explanation must connect that the function model $G$ is increasing for $t > 91$ while actual daily sales are decreasing, so the absolute difference between actual and predicted values grows each day after $t = 91$
parts.scoring_notes: Requires an implicit or explicit connection between "function model is increasing" and "daily sales are decreasing." On day $t = 91$, actual sales and $G(91)$ agree; for $t > 91$ the gap widens because $G$ continues to increase while actual sales fall.
